hypotheses: The output 'z' in TopModule is being asserted at the wrong time relative to the 3-cycle window, possibly due to incorrect update ordering or logic in the state machine [evidence: RTL L41-L45 'if ((w_count + w) == 2'b10) ... z <= 1'b1; ... else z <= 1'b0;']

The state machine may be incorrectly handling the reset signal, causing state or counter variables to be misaligned after a reset event [evidence: waveform_excerpt signal=tb.reset time=1035,1045,2330,2340,2920,2930; RTL L18-L23]
The logic for incrementing w_count and cycle_cnt may be causing off-by-one errors, especially at the window boundary, leading to incorrect z output [evidence: RTL L35-L55, especially L38, L42, L47]

The testbench applies resets asynchronously and at non-regular intervals, which may not be handled robustly by the DUT, leading to mismatches [evidence: testbench L50, waveform_excerpt signal=tb.reset]
The DUT may not be ignoring input 's' after the initial A->B transition, potentially causing unintended state transitions [evidence: testbench L56, stimulus_gen L55-57]
likely_failure_points: The logic in STATE_B for updating z and resetting w_count/cycle_cnt at the end of the 3-cycle window (RTL L35-L49) [evidence: RTL L35-L49]
The handling of reset and initialization of state, w_count, and cycle_cnt (RTL L18-L23) [evidence: waveform_excerpt signal=tb.reset, RTL L18-L23]
The calculation of (w_count + w) and its use in determining z (RTL L41-L42) [evidence: RTL L41-L42]
Potential mishandling of s input after the first transition (RTL L27-L31, testbench L56) [evidence: testbench L56, RTL L27-L31]
recommended_probes: Add probes to w_count, cycle_cnt, and state in TopModule to observe their values at and around failure time 1090
Add a probe to z in TopModule and compare with z_ref in the testbench at each clock edge
Add a probe to s and w in the testbench to correlate input stimulus with state transitions and z output
Add a probe to check if the state machine ever leaves STATE_B after the initial transition (i.e., ensure s is ignored after first transition)
Add a probe to capture the effect of reset on all internal registers in TopModule, especially during asynchronous resets
analysis_notes: The failure is a functional mismatch on output 'z', with 10 mismatches out of 1414 samples, first at time 1090 [evidence: log_excerpt L9-L13]. The waveform excerpt shows reset toggling at several points, including just before the first failure, suggesting possible reset handling issues. The RTL logic for updating z at the end of the 3-cycle window is complex and may be susceptible to off-by-one or ordering errors. The testbench applies resets and randomizes s and w, which could expose edge cases. There is a hint in the testbench about ignoring s after the A->B transition, which may not be fully respected in the DUT. Evidence is sufficient to focus on the window boundary logic and reset handling, but internal signal probes are needed to confirm the exact root cause.
